summary: A small two-story rectangular house with layered materials: sandstone walls with horizontal wooden planks between floors, narrow continuous windows on both levels, corner stonebrick pillars, a stepped brick gable roof, and a white slab/cap base surrounding the footprint.

shape.overall_form: compact rectangular box with a symmetrical gable on the short side and a stepped brick roof
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical across the central ridge (front/back symmetry visible)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: white base/trim (likely quartz or smooth stone slabs)

elements: continuous narrow windows on each floor (runs horizontally across facade)
corner vertical stonebrick pillars spanning floors
horizontal wooden plank band separating the two floors
white slab base platform surrounding building
stepped brick gable roof with central ridge cap
rebuild_hints: Footprint: rectangular; place a one-block thick white slab border (quartz-like) around base before building walls.
Walls: use sandstone for exterior walls; add full-height corner pillars of stonebrick at both front corners.
Floors: create two storeys of equal height; insert a continuous horizontal band of wooden planks between floors and a thin white slab course above the top floor under the roof eaves.
Windows: on each floor leave a continuous row of 1-block-high glass panes/windows across the facade with small vertical separations; align windows on both floors.
Roof: construct a symmetrical stepped gable using brick blocks rising to a central ridge; mirror the gable on the opposite short side.
uncertainties: Exact block types for white base slabs — appears like quartz slabs but could be smooth stone slabs.
Exact wood type for the horizontal band (looks like oak planks) — wood species uncertain.
Interior layout, door location, and rear elevation details are not visible.
Precise external dimensions measured in blocks are estimated from visible proportions; actual counts may differ by ±2 blocks.
